data.title: Freeport-McMoRan tops growth factor grades among S&P materials holdings
data.description:
The S&P 500 has experienced a challenging two-week period marked by escalating geopolitical tensions, sector rotation into defensives, and technical deterioration.

In light of this, below is a list of the top 10 S&P Materials holdings arranged according to their growth factor grade.

The list is topped by Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) and Newmont Corporation (NEM), both sharing the highest growth grade of A-. These two companies represent the leading growth prospects within the top Materials holdings, with both also earning Strong Buy quant ratings.

The middle and lower portions of the list show considerably weaker growth characteristics. Nucor (NUE) and Ecolab (ECL) each carry C- grades, while several holdings including Martin Marietta Materials, Inc. (MLM), Corteva, Inc. (CTVA), CRH plc (CRH), and Linde plc (LIN) display D grades. Air Products and Chemical (APD) and Sherwin-Williams round out the bottom with a D- growth grade each.

The growth factor grade is a quantitative assessment used in stock analysis to evaluate a company’s growth prospects and expansion trajectory. This metric systematically analyzes multiple growth-related indicators to determine how effectively a company is scaling its business operations.

Growth factor grades are typically expressed on a letter scale (A+ through F), where higher grades indicate stronger growth characteristics relative to sector peers. An A+ grade represents exceptional growth potential, while lower grades suggest more moderate expansion or potential headwinds.

Here is the list:

  1. Freeport-McMoRan (FCX), Growth grade: A-

  2. Newmont (NEM), Growth grade: A-

  3. Nucor (NUE), Growth grade: C-

  4. Ecolab (ECL), Growth grade: C-

  5. Martin Marietta Materials (MLM), Growth grade: D

  6. Corteva (CTVA), Growth grade: D

  7. CRH (CRH), Growth grade: D

  8. Linde (LIN), Growth grade: D

  9. Air Products and Chemicals (APD), Growth grade: D-

  10. The Sherwin-Williams Company (SHW), Growth grade: D-

data.title: Wheaton Precious Metals CEO remains bullish on gold long-term
data.description:
Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM) CEO Randy Smallwood remains bullish on gold (XAUUSD:CUR) over the long term, even as shares of his company fell about 3% following the release of fourth-quarter earnings that beat expectations across the board.

Despite recent dollar (DXY) strength driven by geopolitical tensions, Smallwood expressed confidence in gold’s (XAUUSD:CUR) resilience, noting the metal has stayed solidly above $5,000 an ounce.

In an interview with CNBC, Smallwood acknowledged that the conflict in the Middle East has triggered a typical rush into U.S. dollars, similar to what occurred at the start of the Ukraine conflict. However, he expects this pattern to reverse.

“I think over time, what we’ll see is, again, a shift away from U.S. dollars back towards gold over the next few weeks,” Smallwood said.

The CEO, who described himself as “a bit of a gold bug,” said gold has exceeded even his expectations given the current strength of the U.S. dollar.

“I think gold has actually shown strong resilience, you know, staying solidly over $5,000 an ounce,” he noted, adding that he would have a hard time seeing gold fall below that level.

Central banks around the world continue to accumulate gold despite record-high prices, Smallwood observed, emphasizing that this demand shows no signs of slowing down.

Smallwood pointed to what he called a “paradigm shift” in how gold (XAUUSD:CUR) and silver (XAGUSD:CUR) relate to global fiat currencies, with the US dollar’s (DXY) decades-long dominance in central bank reserves coming to an end.

“Gold is now getting to be a larger portion than even the U.S. dollar in terms of central bank reserves,” he explained.

Looking ahead, Smallwood sees significant room for continued growth, noting that gold currently makes up only about half of what has historically been the normal average for central bank reserves.

“And that trend hasn’t changed. And so, we still see that feeding forward,” he said.

Gold and Gold Miner ETFs: (GLD), (IAU), (SGOL), (OUNZ), (BAR), (GDX), (GDXJ), (NUGT), (RING), and (DUST).

data.title: Butler National outlines strategy to expand certified aerospace offerings amid record 27% revenue surge
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Butler National Corporation (BUKS) Q3 2026

Management View

  • Christopher Reedy, CEO, highlighted "record performance for Butler National. The surge in operating and net income of 129% and 98%, respectively, on a 27% increase in revenue demonstrates the efforts Butler team is expanding to drive results through product sales mix and efficiencies." Reedy attributed the growth primarily to the Aerospace Products segment, which saw a 50% revenue increase in the quarter, driven by significant contracts for modifying large airplanes and favorable sales mix of shipped kits.
  • Reedy noted ongoing R&D for new products and that bookings and future business activity have "decreased slightly for the period," attributing the dip to timing of contract receipts while maintaining a positive outlook on ongoing demand.
  • The Tempe Special Mission Electronics team maintained output despite a customer holiday closure and is focused on delivering new gun controls for the M134 Minigun.
  • Professional Services segment remained essentially flat, with local economic factors continuing to impact legacy gaming results. However, sports wagering contributed positively.
  • Adam Sefchick, CFO, stated, "Total revenue for the quarter was $26.9 million compared to $21.2 million same quarter last year, up 27%... net income for the quarter was $6.7 million, up from $3.4 million same quarter last year. Operating income for the quarter was $9.2 million, up from $4 million same quarter last year. Earnings per share for the quarter was $0.10, up from $0.05 prior quarter."
  • Sefchick added, "Our backlog remains strong at $37 million... we did purchase 222,168 shares of our outstanding common stock during the third quarter."

Outlook

  • Management described the backlog as "a solid pipeline of contracted work, repeat customers and engineering programs" as of January 31, 2026.
  • The company continues to focus on a balanced mix of development and repeatable product offerings to strengthen revenue predictability.
  • Yowell, Executive Chairman, stated, "We evaluate strategic opportunities on an ongoing basis... our priority is to pursue opportunities that enhance our technical capabilities, expand our product offerings or provide access to attractive customer channels, particularly within aerospace."
  • The long-term objective is "to expand the number of certified products and modification packages that can be installed repeatedly across aircraft platforms. Each successful STC effectively becomes a long-lived asset for the company, often generating revenue opportunities for many years."

Financial Results

  • Aerospace segment revenue was $17.1 million, up from $11.4 million in the same quarter last year.
  • Professional Services and Gaming segment revenue was $9.9 million, nearly flat year-over-year.
  • Operating margin reached 34% in Q3 2026, compared to 19% in Q3 2025.
  • Cost controls were evident with costs and expenses decreasing as a percentage of revenue compared to the prior year.
  • Share repurchases continued in the quarter.

Q&A

  • Analyst asked about backlog reliability for future revenue. Reedy responded that the backlog is based on contracted sales and includes projects extending over multiple quarters or years, with recurring installation opportunities supporting revenue consistency.
  • On customer concentration risk, Sefchick stated, "Customer concentration is something we monitor closely... we've been intentionally broadening our customer base geographically and across mission types to reduce reliance on any single relationship."
  • Regarding potential acquisitions, Yowell said, "Our priority is to pursue opportunities that enhance our technical capabilities... we are patient and selective."
  • On engineering talent, Reedy explained investments in retention and recruitment, including hiring program managers and developing internal talent.
  • For Boot Hill’s strategic role, Sefchick said, "Boot Hill plays an important role in generating consistent cash flow for the company... Our approach is to manage the property efficiently, invest thoughtfully in the guest experience and preserve its ability to support long-term contractual commitments."
  • Addressing the shift toward repeatable product revenue, Reedy emphasized expanding certified products and modification packages, aiming for consistency and improved operating leverage.
  • On competitive differentiation, Yowell cited "focus and expertise" in specialized modifications, vertical integration, and a diversified operating model.
  • The impact of predictive markets on sports wagering revenue was described as modest, with management viewing new platforms as complementary rather than direct competitors.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' questions focused on sustainability of backlog, customer concentration, M&A strategy, and the evolving nature of aerospace revenues, reflecting a neutral to slightly positive tone, with interest in risk management and strategic direction.
  • Management maintained a confident and disciplined tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently emphasizing structural improvements, operational efficiencies, and long-term strategic objectives.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s confidence appeared to increase, using phrases like "we remain positive about the level of our business activity" and "these quarterly revenue and operating income results are company records."
  • No defensive or evasive responses were detected from management, and analyst sentiment remained consistent quarter-over-quarter, focusing on clarity around growth drivers and risk.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue growth accelerated from 9% in Q2 2026 to 27% in Q3 2026, attributed to strong gains in Aerospace Products.
  • Net income and operating income rose more sharply in Q3 2026, with operating margin higher than the prior quarter.
  • Backlog decreased from $46.3 million in Q2 to $37 million in Q3, with management attributing this to timing of contract receipts but maintaining a positive outlook.
  • Guidance language shifted to emphasize repeatable product revenue and certified offerings, whereas the previous quarter focused more on new product development and STC milestones.
  • Analyst focus remained on core aerospace growth, backlog reliability, customer diversification, and margin sustainability.
  • Management expressed greater confidence in sustaining performance, highlighting the structural nature of recent improvements.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged a slight decrease in bookings and future business activity, attributed to timing of contract awards.
  • Local economic challenges continue to impact the gaming segment, specifically related to the cattle and agricultural industries.
  • Customer concentration risk is being managed through geographic and mission-type diversification.
  • The competitive landscape in aerospace, particularly in engineering talent, is addressed through investment in retention and recruitment.
  • The rise of prediction markets offers both challenges and opportunities for the sports wagering segment but is not viewed as a significant direct threat currently.

Final Takeaway

Management reported another record quarter, led by a 27% revenue increase and significant margin expansion driven by Aerospace Products. The company is prioritizing the development of certified, repeatable offerings to support revenue consistency and long-term growth, while maintaining disciplined cost controls, strong cash flows from Boot Hill, and ongoing stock repurchases. Backlog remains robust despite a sequential decrease, with management expressing confidence in future business activity and risk mitigation strategies across customer concentration and workforce challenges. The company's strategic focus continues to be on organic growth, selective acquisitions, and broadening its base of recurring revenue opportunities in aerospace.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Century Casinos outlines path to higher EBITDAR and cash flow in 2026 as Missouri and Nugget properties drive growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Century Casinos (CNTY) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Peter Hoetzinger, Vice Chairman, Co-CEO & President, announced "full year adjusted EBITDAR increasing 3% year-over-year," despite regulatory headwinds, and highlighted that "excluding the sports betting income in Colorado and the Poland impact, EBITDAR would have increased by 5%, driven by strong performances in Missouri and a nice rebound at Mountaineer in West Virginia." He added that net operating revenue for the fourth quarter was flat due to weather, but adjusted EBITDAR was up 13%.
  • Hoetzinger detailed double-digit EBITDAR growth at multiple properties, naming Colorado, Mountaineer in West Virginia, Caruthersville, Missouri, and the Nugget in Reno, where "EBITDAR was up 21%."
  • Erwin Haitzmann, Chairman & Co-CEO, attributed Caruthersville’s success to its ability to attract high-value customers and said, "With the transition to the permanent casino and hotel building accomplished in November '24, Caruthersville is now an almost $25 million EBITDAR property, effectively more than doubling EBITDAR within the last 6 years."
  • Haitzmann noted that Cape Girardeau saw EBITDAR decrease but maintained strong amenities and customer draw. The new hotel, Starbucks, and a retail sports book were highlighted as recent investments.
  • Haitzmann stated, "At the start of 2025, we eliminated table games at Century Casino Cripple Creek and Central City. This turned out to be the right move. At both properties, the loss of table games revenue was more than offset by payroll savings."
  • Haitzmann reported improved performance at Mountaineer in West Virginia, with cost-saving initiatives driving EBITDAR growth, and discussed challenging weather at Rocky Gap and the Nugget’s continued transition and optimism for 2026.
  • Haitzmann said, "In 2025, the slot coin-in was up 4%, net operating revenue up 2% in local currency and EBITDAR up 1% to $20.3 million" in Alberta.
  • In Poland, Haitzmann explained that "the challenging period marked by license delays and relocation has ended... All current licenses are valid through at least 2028, and we expect stable operations going forward."
  • Hoetzinger provided balance sheet details, reporting cash and cash equivalents at $69 million and total debt outstanding at $338 million, with net debt of $269 million and a net debt-to-EBITDAR ratio of 6.9x, unchanged from the third quarter. He emphasized, "We have no debt maturities for 3 years from now, that is until Q2 of 2029."
  • Hoetzinger offered an outlook for 2026, stating, "We see a good path forward to higher EBITDAR and cash flow for 2026 and beyond."
  • CFO Margaret Stapleton was not directly quoted in this transcript.

Outlook

  • Hoetzinger projected "a strong improvement at the Nugget and the continued ramp of the new land-based facility in Caruthersville" in 2026. He added, "We expect our cash flow to benefit from decreasing CapEx. Whilst we spent a total of $18 million of our cash in 2025, we expect that to come down to between $14 million and $15 million for this year."
  • Hoetzinger stated, "Net operating revenue and adjusted EBITDAR are up significantly compared to last year. Every single property in the U.S. and Canada is showing double-digit EBITDAR growth, especially highlighting great performances at both Colorado Casinos, the Nugget as well as Rocky Gap and Canada."
  • He noted, "Consumer benefits from tax cuts in the OBBB should be additional catalyst in 2026 to drive growth throughout the rest of the year."

Financial Results

  • Haitzmann reported, "EBITDAR in Q4 increased from $4.9 million to $6.1 million" at Caruthersville, and for 2025, "grew from $19 million to $24.4 million."
  • Cape Girardeau’s EBITDAR in Q4 decreased from $6.8 million to $5.9 million, and for the year, from $25.6 million to $24.7 million.
  • At Cripple Creek, EBITDAR in Q4 increased from $1.1 million to $1.5 million. Central City saw EBITDAR in Q4 rise from $0.5 million to $0.7 million.
  • Mountaineer’s EBITDAR in Q4 increased from $2.6 million to $3 million, and for the year from $13.1 million to $14.1 million.
  • Rocky Gap’s EBITDAR in Q4 declined from $3.2 million to $2.9 million, and for the year from $14 million to $13.2 million.
  • The Nugget’s EBITDAR in Q4 increased from $1.1 million to $1.3 million, with the full year declining from $9.7 million to $9.1 million.
  • In Alberta, full year EBITDAR was $20.3 million, up 1%. In Poland, Q4 EBITDAR was up 245% to $0.9 million.

Q&A

  • Jordan Bender, Citizens JMP Securities, LLC, Research Division: Asked about "green shoots" in the retail player segment. Haitzmann responded, "I think we can say that the retail customer is coming back all across the board. We see increases in the retail performance."
  • Bender then inquired about Canada and oil prices. Haitzmann replied, "No, we haven't. Neither did we see less business because of higher oil or gas prices."
  • Ryan Sigdahl, Craig-Hallum Capital Group LLC: Asked for confirmation on "double-digit growth at every U.S. property" for Q1. Haitzmann confirmed, "yes, you heard right with double digits... we have no reason to believe that this should not continue until the rest of the year."
  • Sigdahl questioned the Nugget’s corporate pipeline. Haitzmann replied, "we were able to secure a few large conferences for the years 2030, '31 and '32... in-the-year for-the-year, we will be performing better than in 2025. At the moment, we're up like 15%."
  • Chad Beynon, Macquarie Research: Asked about the impact of the court ruling on video lottery terminal games. Haitzmann answered, "We think this will be definitely good for our casinos. No doubt about it."
  • Beynon also asked about promotional environment and volatility. Haitzmann said, "Nothing that would be worth pointing out that would be out of the, so to speak, ordinary."
  • Jeffrey Stantial, Stifel: Inquired about initiatives to drive visitation in Missouri and cost stabilization. Haitzmann noted, "Caruthersville is a model property when it comes to cost... there is still some room for both... in both properties of Missouri, and we are marketing those heavily."
  • Stantial questioned the weather impact. Haitzmann replied, "In Q4, as we mentioned, a few properties were hit in December a little bit. And in the first quarter, so far, not really anything to mention."
  • Connor Parks, CBRE Securities, LLC: Asked about capital allocation and share repurchases versus debt paydown. Hoetzinger stated, "our main focus would be on debt paydown vis-a-vis share repurchases... going forward, it's definitely in debt paydown."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts directed questions toward sustainability of growth, effects of regulatory changes, and capital allocation, with a neutral and constructive tone, seeking clarity on ongoing trends and management’s confidence in forward guidance.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic stance throughout, frequently emphasizing positive momentum and using terms like "no doubt about it" and "all signs positive," especially in response to outlook and property-specific growth.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone remained steady, maintaining a focus on operational drivers and capital allocation, while management’s confidence increased, underscored by repeated mentions of double-digit growth and sustainable improvements across the portfolio.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 was reiterated and described in more specific terms, with emphasis on decreasing CapEx and harvesting recent investments, while the strategic review process advanced to the point where some asset exclusivity agreements are now in place.
  • Double-digit EBITDAR growth in the current quarter was highlighted as broad-based across the U.S. and Canada, compared to more mixed results in the prior quarter, where segment-level disparities and headwinds from Poland and weather were more significant.
  • Analysts maintained focus on property performance, capital allocation, and strategic review, with ongoing interest in the impact of regulatory changes and promotional dynamics.
  • Management’s tone shifted toward increased optimism, particularly around property-level success stories and expectations for sustained growth in 2026.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited weather-related disruptions, particularly at Rocky Gap and in December, as a recurring risk.
  • Licensing, relocation, and regulatory uncertainty in Poland were previously a headwind but are now described as resolved for the medium term.
  • The strategic review remains ongoing, with divestitures possible but not finalized, limiting management’s ability to discuss future plans in detail.
  • Analysts expressed interest in the impact of court rulings on Missouri operations and the ongoing competitive promotional environment.

Final Takeaway

Century Casinos concluded the year with strong EBITDAR growth despite external challenges, reporting significant improvements at key properties and broad-based momentum across its U.S. and Canadian portfolio. With investments in property upgrades and amenities now largely completed, management emphasized confidence in a path to higher EBITDAR and cash flow in 2026, supported by lower CapEx requirements, continued operational efficiencies, and the ramp-up of recently enhanced assets. The ongoing strategic review may impact the company’s asset base, but management's focus remains on driving organic growth and prioritizing debt reduction in the near term.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: ImmunityBio rises after updates on cell therapy programs
data.description:
ImmunityBio (IBRX) shares gained on Friday after the cancer drug developer announced the successful conclusion of two manufacturing engineering programs designed to validate its Cytokine-Enriched NK Cell (m-ceNK) Platform.

The NK2022 and NK2023 programs involving 64 cancer patients and healthy subjects were designed to assess the feasibility of large-volume, non-mobilized leukapheresis, a procedure that separates white blood cells from blood.

The programs also assessed how downstream NK cell enrichment and cytokine-driven memory programming can be reproduced in both healthy donors and cancer patients.

According to ImmunityBio (IBRX), all subjects completed the apheresis collection with no serious adverse events linked to the procedure, and 10 cancer patients received their collected cells post-NK cell enrichment process.

Thanks to data from NK2022 and NK2023, the company said it could start QUILT-3.076, a Phase 1 trial designed to evaluate its immunotherapy inbakicept-pmln (Anktiva) with autologous M-ceNK cells in solid tumors.

As part of QUILT-3.076, 10 patients have already received weekly M-ceNK infusions with Anktiva administered subcutaneously once every two weeks, ImmunityBio (IBRX) said.

data.title: The markets are anticipating the U.S. is looking for an off ramp from the Iran war – analyst
data.description:
Markets are currently pricing in an expectation that the U.S. is actively seeking a way out of the conflict with Iran, according to Adam Crisafulli, founder and president of Vital Knowledge.

“Markets anticipate that the U.S. is looking for an off ramp. It’s looking for an exit strategy,” Crisafulli said in an interview with CNBC.

Despite the severity of the geopolitical situation, the market’s reaction has been relatively measured. The S&P 500 (SP500) has declined approximately 3% since the war began, which Crisafulli described as “a sizable decline, but it’s actually very impressive considering the magnitude of what’s happening geopolitically.”

This restraint reflects the market’s ongoing struggle to balance geopolitical risk against the potential for sudden relief.

However, investors remain concerned about obstacles to a resolution. While Crisafulli noted that markets are not doubting the administration’s desire to end the conflict, the focus has shifted to “his ability to” do so as Iran continues to exert pressure on the Strait of Hormuz. This ongoing aggression represents the primary concern weighing on investor sentiment.

The S&P 500 (SP500) is currently sitting at the low end of its recent trading range, around 6,700.

“I think people are very nervous the longer we sit on this level, the higher the odds that we break down through it,” Crisafulli warned. A sustained breakdown below this level could signal further deterioration in market conditions.

Beneath the surface of the major indexes, significant dispersion among sectors continues to create volatility. Crisafulli suggested that a market rotation could potentially push the S&P 500 (SP500) back toward the middle or upper end of its range at around 7,000.

“The dispersion is also playing a big role too,” he noted.

Investors remain alert to any headline that could spark a relief rally, with the sense that a diplomatic breakthrough could emerge at any moment.

“There definitely is in the back of people’s minds a sense that we could see at any moment a headline,” Crisafulli said, referencing the president’s comments to reporters throughout the week as a source of ongoing market attention.

S&P 500 ETFs: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), (VFFSX), and (SWPPX).

data.title: Bahrain and Saudi Arabia Formula One likely to be cancelled due to Mideast conflict
data.description:
Heighted security risks in the Middle East are likely to force the cancellation of Formula One (FWONA) (FWONB) (FWONK) races in Bahrain and Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, to be held next month.

According to media reports, a final decision has not yet been made, but it is unlikely either race will be held due to their proximity to the conflict in Iran and travel challenges into the region. The Bahrain International Circuit is located just 22 km (13.67 mi) from a U.S. naval facility in Bahrain that was recently targeted by Iranian missiles and drones.  

With the Bahrain race scheduled for April 10-12 and the Saudi Arabian event the following weekend, event organizers must consider the timeline for aggressions in the region. With both countries facing continued missile and drone attacks from Tehran, including the U.S. Embassy in Riyadh and fuel tanks in Bahrain, the risk to spectators and racing teams may be deemed untenable under current conditions. The events typically draw crowds of at least 100K over the entire weekend.

Should the events be cancelled, it is unlikely they will be rescheduled given the lack of availability in what is already a very tight Formula One schedule. However, race organizers typically insure these events for as much as $100M in the event of cancellation.

F1A, the entity that manages Formula One, has not yet responded to Seeking Alpha’s request for either confirmation of the cancellations or whether the events have been insured.

data.title: Recession odds climb as Middle East conflict escalates, according to prediction markets
data.description:
Rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are beginning to weigh on investor sentiment, with prediction markets signaling a growing concern about the idea of an economic recession.

Data from prediction market platform Kalshi shows the implied probability of the United States entering a recession this year has climbed to roughly 35.3%. That represents a notable increase from late February, when the odds stood near 21.6% just before hostilities between the United States and Iran kicked off. 

Financial markets have responded with heightened volatility as traders assess the potential economic consequences of the conflict. Escalating geopolitical risk has historically pressured equities by increasing uncertainty around global trade, energy markets, and monetary policy.

Since the close of trading on February 27—just before the conflict started—major U.S. stock indexes have moved lower. The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) has declined approximately 4.8% over that period, reflecting broad investor caution. 

Meanwhile, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) has fallen about 3.3%, while the technology-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) has slipped roughly 2.3% as investors reassess risk exposure.

While the U.S. economy has remained relatively resilient in recent months, the sharp rise in recession probabilities highlights growing concern among market participants that prolonged geopolitical tensions could begin to weigh more heavily on growth, corporate earnings, and overall financial conditions. 

Dow ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (UDOW), (DOG), (DXD), and (SDOW). 

S&P 500 ETFs: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), (VFFSX), and (SWPPX). 

Nasdaq ETFs: (QQQ), (QQQM), (SQQQ), (TQQQ), (QLD), and (QID).

data.title: Wall Street slides as investors assess PCE inflation and GDP data
data.description:
Wall Street’s major indexes moved lower on Friday as investors digested a fresh batch of economic data, highlighted by the latest PCE inflation and GDP reports.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.4%, the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -0.1%, and the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.8%. 

From a sector-by-sector stance, six of the 11 S&P sectors were in the green with utilities being the strongest performing area on the session while industrials have been the weakest. 

On the economic front, core PCE inflation rose 3.1% Y/Y in January, a touch hotter than expected. Furthermore, the U.S. GDP growth estimate was revised lower to +0.7% in Q4. 

Additionally, durable goods orders growth has stalled in the month of January. The January job openings increased more than expected in the latest JOLTS report, and consumer sentiment data declined more than expected in March

U.S. Treasury yields were muted. The 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) was 2 basis points higher at 3.74%, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was at 4.29%, and the 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) was at 4.91%. 

As for stocks that are on the move, Darden Restaurants (DRI) climbed +1.7%, and shares of Adobe (ADBE) fell -6.1%.

data.title: Surf Air Mobility outlines 20–30% revenue growth target for 2026 with platform pivot and BETA Technologies partnership
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Surf Air Mobility Inc. (SRFM) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Deanna White stated the company is moving from a stabilization phase into "growth mode," highlighting a 20% to 30% increase in 2026 revenue guidance compared to the prior year. This guidance does not include early electric aircraft deployment, which is expected to contribute to revenue and efficiencies in 2027 through a new partnership with BETA Technologies.
  • White described the year as a "dramatic step change in operational performance," noting, "We achieved our guidance of profitability in our Airline Operations for the full year of 2025, defined as positive adjusted EBITDA." She emphasized strategic initiatives such as the launch of "Powered by Surf On Demand" and "Surf On Demand Cargo," both beginning to generate profitable revenue in 2025.
  • The company raised over $100 million in equity during 2025, which "substantially reduced our overall cost of capital and lower our net debt," providing flexibility to pursue growth.
  • Surf Air Mobility secured a firm order for 25 BETA electric aircraft, with options for 75 more, and reached an agreement to become BETA’s exclusive factory authorized service center in Hawaii. White said, "We anticipate that the improved unit economics of BETA electric aircraft will lead to increased profitability in our scheduled service and on-demand charter businesses over time."
  • Chief Financial Officer Oliver Reeves reported, "Net debt decreased 47% from $139 million at December 31, 2024, to $74 million at December 31, 2025. This decrease in net debt also benefited from the conversion of $48 million in convertible notes, inclusive of interest."

Outlook

  • For 2026, Surf Air Mobility projects revenue in the range of $128 million to $138 million and adjusted EBITDA loss in the range of $40 million to $50 million. CFO Reeves said, "Our revenue guidance range of 20% to 30% year-over-year revenue growth for fiscal year 2026 contemplates accelerating growth in our on-demand charter business and partial year revenue contribution for SurfOS."
  • Revenue growth is expected to be "heavily weighted to the back half of 2026" due to SurfOS commercialization and on-demand charter expansion. The company expects adjusted EBITDA loss and margins to improve each quarter on a comparable year-over-year basis.
  • First quarter 2026 revenue is anticipated to range from $24 million to $26 million, with adjusted EBITDA loss between $15.5 million and $13.5 million.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter 2025 revenue was $26.4 million, within guidance, and marked an eighth consecutive quarter meeting or exceeding guidance. This represented a 9% sequential decrease, attributed to the exit of unprofitable routes, offset by an 8% increase in on-demand charter revenue.
  • Full year 2025 revenue totaled $106.6 million, an 11% decrease from 2024, primarily due to a 15% decline in scheduled service revenue, partially offset by a 3% increase in on-demand charter revenue.
  • Adjusted EBITDA loss for Q4 2025 was just under $8 million, a 19% improvement sequentially. Full year adjusted EBITDA loss improved 5% year-over-year to $41.7 million.
  • Operational improvements included a controllable completion factor of 98% in Q4 2025, compared to 89% in Q4 2024. On-time departures and arrivals also improved year-over-year.

Q&A

  • Amit Dayal, H.C. Wainwright: Asked about SurfOS investment and rollout. CEO White explained the go-to-market strategy and noted, "The bulk of that revenue that we're going to get this year from commercializing SurfOS is going to occur in the second half of the year."
  • Dayal inquired about the BETA partnership’s revenue impact. White clarified, "We cannot take deliveries and put them into full commercial service until the aircraft is certified, but we have plans to start with the CTOL cargo and move to the passenger variant."
  • Dayal sought details on BETA aircraft economics. White stated, "We anticipate that there'll be 30% more improvement in operating costs. Where that will come from is 2 areas: fuel and also maintenance."
  • Dayal asked about the balance sheet post-investment. CFO Reeves responded, "We believe these investments will generate significant ROI, and it will create shareholder value."
  • David Storms, Stonegate: Queried Airline Operations profitability drivers. White said technology and electric aircraft adoption are key, with a "goal to become a premier MRO for the electric aircraft."
  • Storms probed BETA partnership geographic expansion. White declined specifics for competitive reasons but emphasized the advantage of an existing network for rapid adoption.
  • Storms asked about early traction in Surf On Demand. White reported a "big uptick" and a pipeline of independent brokers for the program.
  • Austin Moeller, Canaccord: Asked about timing for BrokerOS and SurfOS revenue. White projected first half of 2026, with more significant revenue in the second half.
  • Moeller inquired about Caravan Electrification. Co-Founder Sudhin Shahani confirmed continued pursuit of partnerships, with no intention to self-fund the program.
  • Investor-submitted questions touched on topics including the shift from in-house electrification, technical certification hurdles for electric aircraft, revenue segment profitability, and SurfOS integration with OEMs and eVTOL manufacturers. Management confirmed ongoing discussions with multiple industry stakeholders.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a positive tone, expressing congratulatory remarks and focusing on growth areas, partnerships, and operational gains. Questions were largely constructive, seeking clarity on execution timelines and economic impact.
  • Management exhibited confidence and strategic clarity, repeatedly referencing "growth mode," "pivoting to growth," and "significant investment priorities." Phrases like "We anticipate" and "We believe" signaled optimism tempered by acknowledgment of certification dependencies.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management sustained a positive to slightly more optimistic sentiment, with greater emphasis on growth and platform transition.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from stabilization and transformation to "pivoting to growth" and a platform-centric strategy.
  • The introduction of a 20% to 30% revenue growth target for 2026, a substantial increase from previous guidance, was a notable change.
  • Management’s focus shifted from capital structure optimization and cost control to scaling partnerships, platform deployment, and electric aircraft adoption.
  • Analysts’ questions evolved from concerns about route exits and cost reductions to inquiries about SurfOS commercialization, electric aircraft economics, and growth drivers.
  • Management tone moved from cautious optimism to more assertive confidence regarding execution and path to profitability.

Risks and Concerns

  • Key challenges cited included certification timelines for electric aircraft, which remain the "largest hurdle" before commercial flights.
  • Management no longer plans to invest $50 million to $100 million in the Caravan Electrification program, instead pursuing partnerships to reduce capital risk.
  • The company is making "significant investments" in SurfOS and Hawaii, reflected in projected adjusted EBITDA loss for 2026.
  • Exposure to fuel price volatility is mitigated by operating a fuel-efficient fleet, with future benefits anticipated from electric aircraft.

Final Takeaway

Surf Air Mobility’s Q4 2025 call underscores a decisive transition from financial and operational stabilization to aggressive growth, with a 2026 revenue target reflecting 20% to 30% anticipated expansion. The company’s platform-centric strategy, underpinned by proprietary software, expanded on-demand charter programs, and a strategic partnership with BETA Technologies, positions it to capitalize on the next wave of aviation innovation. Management’s focus on operational efficiency, new revenue streams such as MRO services, and disciplined investment in digital infrastructure signals a commitment to capturing a growing share of the advanced air mobility ecosystem in the years ahead.
